title: Securities Trading
definition: Securities Trading lets you buy and sell quoted securities such as shares, treasury bills, and bonds on the Uganda Securities Exchange and other regional exchanges, with additional support services including depository handling, nominee account services, investment research, and trading advisory.
benefits: Access to buying and selling shares, bonds, and treasury bills through a regulated market.
Securities Central Depository (SCD) account support, including electronic record-keeping, easier transfers, and reduced paperwork.
Lower operational risk through electronic custody, reducing loss, theft, or damage of physical certificates.
Professional support through investment research, analysis, and securities trading advisory.
eligibility: To start, open an SCD account and fund your client trust account. For individuals: three coloured passport photos, valid ID (National ID or passport), and a stamped recent bank statement (not more than 3 months). For corporates/SACCOs/NGOs/investment clubs/churches: certificate of incorporation, memorandum and articles of association, TIN certificate, board resolution to open SCD account, three coloured passport photos for each signatory, and valid IDs for signatories.
